column1: Bobby Witt Jr. stands out as the top pick, riding a strong recent stretch and hitting safely in nearly 80% of his games, with a favorable park factor and a manageable matchup against Casey Legumina. Alec Burleson offers similar reliability at home, benefiting from a platoon advantage and steady contact rates. Brandon Marsh is also in play thanks to his consistent approach and a slight park boost. Witt’s blend of consistency and matchup security makes him the most dependable option.
